Author: teo ming yi
Review: Big breakfast at this busy little cafe along the street of Bangsar Village. Like all standard English breakfast, I find them good but nothing to shout about. Something I wouldn’t expect to write about was the toast! Really nice, perfectly …MoreBig breakfast at this busy little cafe along the street of Bangsar Village. Like all standard English breakfast, I find them good but nothing to shout about. Something I wouldn’t expect to write about was the toast! Really nice, perfectly toasted and drenched in margarine! Had one of the best Ice Latte here as well! And most impressive of all was their subtle effort in protecting the environment! No where in the cafe premise or menu were they trying to claim credits for their environmental friendly efforts but they are the first Cafe I ever encountered that is using paper straws for their ice beverages!! There were also no traces of plastics in all their tablewares! Unlike most cafes that put on a pretentious front on protecting the environment by using even more plastics (strawless cup) or by using “no straws” as an excuse to cut cost (not to mention, those cringeworthy loud announcement to the world, like as if people won’t notice..), this cafe silently doing their tiny part in spite of the potential additional costs to them.P.s. the staff were friendly and extremely attentive as well.

Author: Miyako Takajo
Review: First of all, the service is excellent. Many servers welcomed us on my way in and lead me very quickly to our table. The menu had a lot of variety but that did not detract from the quality. I had ordered the big breakfast and it was …MoreFirst of all, the service is excellent. Many servers welcomed us on my way in and lead me very quickly to our table. The menu had a lot of variety but that did not detract from the quality. I had ordered the big breakfast and it was delivered very quickly to my table. The first thing I tried were the mushrooms which were plump, juicy and bursting with flavour. They claimed the hashbrown was homemade and the quality confirmed that with a slight hint of acidity not found in frozen hashbrowns. Their scrambled eggs were exactly what you would expect; you could practically slurp them like noodles. The salad which paired very well with the hashbrown lacked the acidity which would have made the dish somewhat unpleasant. The bacon was lovely, not too salty and a wonderful texture that was not too chewy nor crispy and the fat was kept to a minimum. Finally, the artisan bread was a highlight, being just the right texture and a subtle flavour to finish off on. I highly recommend this place to anyone driving through Bangsar in search of a good plave to eat.
Location: 20, Jalan Telawi 2, Bangsar, Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Author: Hannah R
Review: We used to be regular customers at this restaurant but it has gone seriously downhill. We ordered the whole tandoori chicken which was mostly bone and contained no chicken breast pieces (despite it claiming to be a whole chicken). We …MoreWe used to be regular customers at this restaurant but it has gone seriously downhill. We ordered the whole tandoori chicken which was mostly bone and contained no chicken breast pieces (despite it claiming to be a whole chicken). We mentioned this to a waiter who went to speak to who I assume was the manager. The manager was INCREDIBLY rude and defensive - shouting and talking over us despite there being other customers around. We explained that in the past it has not been like this, and even offered to still pay for it but he huffed and took the food away saying he’ll only charge us for a half chicken. He then came back telling us he can’t resell the pieces as we’ve sliced into them (which we did to see if it was all bone like the other pieces). It is absolutely outrageous that in the middle of a pandemic he would even consider reselling food that had been served to other customers, and showed a total disregard for food hygiene. It is no surprise to me that other customers have found cockroaches in their food. We most certainly will not be returning!
Location: No.8, Level 1, The Sphere No.1, Avenue 1, Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar South, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Ahmed Jagirdar
Review: To call this food Indian cuisine is a disgrace. The food when judged by appearance surely looks delicious but you are soon met with disappointment as you take a bite. My order was butter naan, chicken tikka, Sheekh kebab, butter chicken …MoreTo call this food Indian cuisine is a disgrace. The food when judged by appearance surely looks delicious but you are soon met with disappointment as you take a bite. My order was butter naan, chicken tikka, Sheekh kebab, butter chicken gravy, lamb biryani. The only thing which I can say that was good was the sheekh kebab and the butter naan. The butter chicken tasted literally like tomato puree, the chicken tikka didn't have a strong enough spices/masala and the biryani had a very mild taste. The lamb was very chewy and not tender at all. This is not Indian food. And lastly, the price, ridiculous amount charged for food which was below average and extremely high service charge. Went with a foreigner friend and even they didn't like the food and as an Indian this is horrible Indian food. As an Indian I wouldn't call this Indian food.
Location: No.8, Level 1, The Sphere No.1, Avenue 1, Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar South, Kuala Lumpur

Review: Being an indian you tend to be a little more critical of restaurants selling Indian food abroad. But my reason for giving this establishment a 3 star is not that. But my reason for giving this establishment a 3 star is not that. While the ambience and location earn top scores but the food itself leaves a lot to be desired for especially when compared to other Indian restaurants in Kuala Lumpur. The breads are good but the curries do not have the desired masalas in them and tend to be very westernised in taste. The biryani was also just about ok. All and all of you are nearby and want to have Indian food or something close to it please do make a visit, but definitely not worth the travel.

Author: Will T
Review: myburgerlab has always been crowded with people and knowing that, I was willing to wait for my late dinner. Especially tonight the OUG branch crowd is very low and it is not even a full house. A rare scene in the same place where I stay. I …Moremyburgerlab has always been crowded with people and knowing that, I was willing to wait for my late dinner. Especially tonight the OUG branch crowd is very low and it is not even a full house. A rare scene in the same place where I stay. I walked into the store and waited for the girl at the counter to take my order. She was inattentive as she was looking behind her. There's an awkward deafening 1-forever-minute silence in a loud environment. It must be a long night for them, aish, let's fast forward to paying, finding myself a single seat and expecting a slightly faster turnaround time. I so convinced myself. I was wrong. What's upsetting? Not the wait but the family next door who came to sit right next to me later got their burgers first! Not to mention that I had to literally eyeing the people next door biting into their delicious nasi lemak ayam redang burger that I have yet to taste before; anxiously and patiently waiting till 2150. Yeap, and it was not until I enquired one of the seemingly inattentive staff (for I was sitting alone for quite sometime in an rather empty store) that I got my takeaway. I accept the fact that our burgers are made fresh upon order, but I can't brain the unfair wait in this circumstance. Let's hope my next visit will be a better one because my verdict of the burger: splendid. PS: 2-star for the bad experience. 5 star for the burger. #hangry
Location: 8, Jalan Awan Hijau, Taman OUG, Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Review: Love Kayra. I was a big fan when they first started in Taman Tun and am a certified top fan of their Bangsar Village offering. I was a big fan when they first started in Taman Tun and am a certified top fan of their Bangsar Village offering. The menu is diverse and produces hit after hit of contemporary interpretations of authentic Keralan staples. I’ve been ordering these yummilicious dishes for over 1 year 1. Fry Me Up 2 Ways (egg and sambal heaven) 2. Chicken Dosa Taco 3. Prawn Ulartiyath (current addiction - spicy prawns Bliss) 4. Fish biryani (succulent flesh of the fish in the fragrant aroma of the rice is the best way to conclude the night). 5. The okra dish. 6. Kochin drink (caramelised pineapple and lychee) and 6. Keralan Twist (lime and mint). Can’t recommend Kayra enough ❤️

Author: EmmY tJia

Author: Calvin
Review: One of the best nasi lemak in town. Great taste & affordable price in the city area. Recommend to tried their nasi lemak with fried chicken or rendang chicken. Sambal wise, it taste towards sweetness while if you tried the Nasi Lemak …MoreOne of the best nasi lemak in town. Great taste & affordable price in the city area. Recommend to tried their nasi lemak with fried chicken or rendang chicken. Sambal wise, it taste towards sweetness while if you tried the Nasi Lemak Tanglin it taste towards a bit spicy kampung style.Lunch time will be super crowded at the restaurant. Suggest not to order through the delivery if  you are rushing for something. It may take 45 min  or more time to delivery...Quality of food & service have slightly deteriorated for delivery & taking away since the pandemic. Fried chicken taste a bit dried & not so juicy. So, you cannot compared the freshness to what you have now and dine in over there.

Author: Kristine W

Author: Johnny Lau

Author: Tuck Leong

The best thing is probably the 'crunchy benda' that you can order for extra and mix it into the rice. Even better is the unbeatable …MoreEverything was just, good. Real good. Author: amirul 'arif (capbrad)

Location: 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Mohamad Shazreen Sazali
Review: One of my favorite nasi lemak joint. Good food with affordable prices. Can get very packed depending on time of day. Best to just order take away because, if you know Uptown like I do, you know parking is a serious issue there. However there's the alternative of parking in Starling mall. So that helps.
Location: 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Calvin Lim
Review: The best nasi lemak you can find in this earth. The rice is super fragrant and have a very balance of coconut milk, salt and other seasoning flavor. The fried chicken is super juicy and not dry at all, seasoning is super good and have a …MoreThe best nasi lemak you can find in this earth. The rice is super fragrant and have a very balance of coconut milk, salt and other seasoning flavor. The fried chicken is super juicy and not dry at all, seasoning is super good and have a strong lemongrass smell and taste on it. The price is very fair and the service is very fast after you get your seat.The only downside is that this restaurant is super popular and expect a long queue during lunch hour. If you plan to drive to here to eat, don’t bother driving around and search for parking spot, just park inside The Starling Mall as the first hour is FOC.

Author: Pearlyn Chew
Review: Famous for the Nasi Lemak Ayam Goreng (RM 10.50). Found it abit over-rated, chicken didn’t have much meat, mostly bones. But it was indeed nicely fried. But it was indeed nicely fried. Ask for extra ‘goreng goreng’ (crispy fried bits)Can be abit intimidating for first timers. There is no official queue, seats are self-service. Author: Davina - Anne Shirley

Author: Ace Khaw

Author: zincofer42

Author: joanne chia

Author: Tan Chee Jian
Review: Delicious nasi lemak with fried chicken 😋
Location: 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Larry Chu
Review: This place deserves it's fame. Atmosphere is hectic. Super long queue and crowded . Outddor seating is free seating, you need to "chope" your table before ordering. Indoor seating system is by queue, but super long queue. …MoreThis place deserves it's fame. Atmosphere is hectic. Super long queue and crowded . Outddor seating is free seating, you need to "chope" your table before ordering. Indoor seating system is by queue, but super long queue.The ayam goreng set comes with a nice chiken leg, deep fried with fragrant speices. The rice is superb, fluffy with ample amount of cocunut. The combination of sambal , ikan bilis, peanuts and ayam goreng blended so well that one plate is NOT ENOUGH. However, the queue deters me from ordering another plate. Most of the dishers were sold out on my visit, so I only managed to eat the lontong vege and the okra. tried the ayam rendang as well, but I would prefer the goreng version.Its definitely worth a visit if you have heard of this place  but hesitated in trying it out, GO FOR IT. It took me 2 years to bring myself here as I thought it's just another nasi lemak.Well, in my conclusion this is much better than Adam Road Nasi Lemak (Singapore) which was rumoured to be frequented by royal Brunei family.
Location: 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: W. Gan
Review: Went on a Saturday 7 am the crowd already started. The service is efficient and the fried chicken is crispy but overall the nasi lemak is still under my expectation despite the rating from others are high.
Location: 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Haslinda Haron
Review: Always the place to go for good nasi lemak😃
Location: 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Chan Da-Xia
Review: I like their foods so much. Very very very delicious. Of course the nasi lemak is my favorite. Second is the laksa. The best place that serve nasi lemak. The chicken, the sambal and the rice, everything is great. Just the sambal and another …MoreI like their foods so much. Very very very delicious. Of course the nasi lemak is my favorite. Second is the laksa. The best place that serve nasi lemak. The chicken, the sambal and the rice, everything is great. Just the sambal and another kuah is a bit oily. Their bakso also great. The design of this place is simple but it was a nice place. Clean and comfy. Recommended!

Author: Michelle
Review: The infamous VP nasi lemak ! Always busy always packed to the brim . The queue moves fast and the turnover is fast so fret not. Plentiful of seating upstairs and downstairs. …MoreThe infamous VP nasi lemak ! Always busy always packed to the brim . The queue moves fast and the turnover is fast so fret not. Plentiful of seating upstairs and downstairs.Affordable meal that is consistent in taste! I guess that is why most people go back to them as they know what to expect! Food is served very fast so if you want to grab a quick meal, it is convenient.It may not be the best Nasi Lemak in town but whatever they are serving is definitely good enough to serve the mass market.Many times we have been there, the taste of the chicken has always been the same - it is very well flavored and managed to retain its moistness. The rice is fragrant and most importantly sambal is a decent with a good balance of sweetness and spicyness.The lontong is nice, the very light savoury coconut broth when paired with the kuah lodeh gives a good balance in flavour.The beef rendang is tender and jam packed with spices. Overall a winning combo in my books.

Author: Jit Shiong Yeoh

Author: Randiah Green

Author: Jade Lee

Author: Miki Maru

Author: EeLi

Author: MoonGal

Author: Faruq Arif Tajul Ariffin

Author: Mr. J
Review: Getting a table here is always going to be a challenge since it's almost always fully packed. That should be an indication of how popular this restaurant is. That should be an indication of how popular this restaurant is. You probably won't be able to find parking nearby either, as the restaurant is located in a very busy commercial area. Luckily though, the Starling mall just opened and you can park there (first hour is free) and walk to this restaurant. The specialty here is nasi lemak, and it's the reason why people come here. Not just your everyday people, even people like our Prime Minister and even celebrities (their pictures decorate the walls). The nasi lemak really is great, as is the fried chicken (whole legs only). Author: Ravikumar Aleyak
Review: ONE OF THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EXPERIENCESThis restaurant is definitely over rated in social media which makes it highly …MoreONE OF THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EXPERIENCESThis restaurant is definitely over rated in social media which makes it highly sought after by patrons. It is always known to be packed and patrons had to queue to be seated and get their desired dishes. Business has been definitely great for the owner that all they think of is only profit and totally neglect customer service and satisfaction.On my recent trip, two of us were forced to share a row of table, meant for 12 pax, which is already occupied by 8 pax from two different groups. We requested to sit at other tables, as literally plenty more rows of tables were empty at approximately half of the restaurant, but we were forced to seat at the occupied table. Then came a single patron and he was forced to seat between us and the other patrons on the same table. The poor boy was feeling so uncomfortable throughout.It just does not make sense to  crowd all patrons on same tables to be fully occupied although plenty more tables for half of the restaurant was still empty. I observed the same was happening to all incoming patrons.This shows that all the owner is thinking about is pure profit and maximizing their incomes, to a point that customers comfort and service is totally neglected.From the faces of the many of the patrons there at that time, it was obvious that many experienced the uncomfortable meal time and horrible customer service.Based on many reviews written here, it seems many of the patrons have experienced that same terrible moment over time but nothing has done by the owner to improve the service.They just treat customers terribly.I had one visit there and certainly the last visit.Definitely One of the TOP MOST PLACES NOT TO GO.Note to the Owner:Please go back to basic and understand the simply philosophy that you are not doing a favour to the customers, but on the opposite, it is the customers who are doing you favours by visiting your restaurant. A customer is not an outsider to your business, but actually a main part of your business. If not for all the customers, you would not be making so much of profit.Learn to treat your customers properly. We are not demanding royal treatment, but merely to be treated as human and paying customer.Please treat all these reviews seriously and act accordingly, before it gets too late that people get sick of all these.And please never forget that plenty more restaurants with delicious food and excellent customer services around.Adieu.
Location: 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

The waiters were very attentive. The environment was great very comfy and private a nice date place.The not so great things was the quality of the food. While the prices are somewhat affordable for sashimi etc. Most of the quality wasn't there. The red tuna was definitely of a lower grade and while I wouldn't say it wasn't fresh, it had that soft squishy feel of fish that's been defrosted after a while. The prawn sashimi was alright, it was fresh but not as sweet as most places. It just tasted like lower quality items. But I guess it matches with the price. The sushi rolls as well had extremely soggy seaweed which I feel is an easy fix.Overall it was okay but it's not on par with most fine dining Japanese restaurants.

Author: Azimeer Manaf
Review: MTR is a vegetarian South Indian restaurant located in Brickfields. This restaurant chain started back in 1924 by 3 brothers in Bangalore and the set up a new standard for Indian restaurants by adopting European standard of hygiene and …MoreMTR is a vegetarian South Indian restaurant located in Brickfields. This restaurant chain started back in 1924 by 3 brothers in Bangalore and the set up a new standard for Indian restaurants by adopting European standard of hygiene and cleanliness when the brothers visited Europe.The restaurant in Brickfields continued this standard and it is reflected in their cleanliness. I was super impressed with the washroom, it is very clean and dry, so unlike most asian restaurants. The servers are attentive, polite and fast.Food wise, is consistent and made fresh upon the customer making the order. We had the masala dosa and the rawa masala dosa. The dosas were just heavenly, after lathering it with the clarified butter. It was a definitely filling breakfast. Although I have tasted a better coconut chutney elsewhere. A highly recommend place if you are particular about service and cleanliness, plus to food is good too.
Location: 69, Jalan Thambipillay, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Author: Alex Motion
Review: First of all the menu has 50% Asian foods rather than Italian. We ordered Diavola Pizza, Rendang Fettuccine and Bruschetta. …MoreFirst of all the menu has 50% Asian foods rather than Italian. We ordered Diavola Pizza, Rendang Fettuccine and Bruschetta.The Pizza:The crust was great. However, the pizza was super spicy (it supposed to have some chilli flakes on it). Even the Ragu meat of it was spicy. I don't remember having any pizza this spicy. It is supposed to be Italian food not Indian.  Since we ordered a 2 person package, the pizza portion was quite small compared to their normal oval shape size. They should have mentioned you are going to get a small size pizza and not Regular.The Bruschetta:It came with Sweet sauce and cinnamon on top. What kind of bruschetta uses sweet sauce. This not a dessert, it is appetizer.Overall very bad experience and probably never going to order from them again. My advise to management is to stick to Italian food or change your name to Malay food or South East Asian food.
Location: Block C1, Lot 2, Level G3, Publika Shopping Gallery, No. 1, Jln Dutamas 1, Solaris Dutamas, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Suzlene Zakariah
Review: This risto serves delicious authentic & halal Italian food- we enjoyed the squid ink seafood pasta it's spices hitting you slow, their rectangle margherita & beef pepperoni pizzas were just right- the crispy edges are a winner. Their …MoreThis risto serves delicious authentic & halal Italian food- we enjoyed the squid ink seafood pasta it's spices hitting you slow, their rectangle margherita & beef pepperoni pizzas were just right- the crispy edges are a winner. Their lasagne is full of beef & cheese fillings and we were so full there wasn't any room for dessert. Perhaps another time.Nice & simple decor with pics of fellow Italians Al Pacino, Brando and surprise surprise Jon Bon Jovi on their walls. The only thing they could improve on would be for their servers not too rush us off by wiping the table while I was still finishing my pizza or picking our plates the minute we finish. A bit pricey but for the good food it was worth it. 4 pax averages around RM250++ depending on what you choose.
Location: Block C1, Lot 2, Level G3, Publika Shopping Gallery, No. 1, Jln Dutamas 1, Solaris Dutamas, Kuala Lumpur

It normally takes longer to make risotto from scratch. The seafood is not cooked together with the risotto, sort of steamed separately and decorated on top of the risotto. Author: Saiful Jazri Zahari
Review: When I saw their banner that they're opening, I was excited. Singapore's best Halal Italian restaurant opening in Publika. When I tried it yesterday for iftar I was quite disappointed. …MoreWhen I saw their banner that they're opening, I was excited. Singapore's best Halal Italian restaurant opening in Publika. When I tried it yesterday for iftar I was quite disappointed.Maybe it was the menu I chose or perhaps because I was fasting the whole day but some how, it wasn't up to my expectations.  Touted as the best halal Italian restaurant in Singapore I had high expectations but it fell really short after these dishes.Charbroiled cod fish with potato rosti. The fish was large and moist. Very rare to see a large piece of cod fish with that price. Unfortunately it tasted oily. Perhaps it was due to the butter lemon oil or butter chilli oil but again, cod itself tastes rich and set ontop of oily potato rosti and creamy rocket dressing, well for me it was over the top in richness. What a shame as the fish on its own was quite good.The baked mac and cheese with slice chicken was quite okay. I loved the mozzarella but after a while it got to cheesy for me as well. The  homemade chicken sausage with mushrooms were up to my expectations as it should be but the truffle fries could do with a tad more truffle oil.Dinner was saved by dessert. The molten chocolate  ĺava. Service was fast and friendly, which is a real plus. Layout of the restaurant is not really stroller friendly with steps and. Arrow brjtđàFor the price I paid, I believe there are many more halal Italian restaurants I KL I can go to that is tastier with the same price. Will I return? Probably to try other menu but if the result is similar, I'll give this restaurant a miss.
Location: Block C1, Lot 2, Level G3, Publika Shopping Gallery, No. 1, Jln Dutamas 1, Solaris Dutamas, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Yih Yang Chee
Review: Had to check it out after hearing good things about them.Was underwhelmed by the small portions. The pricing suffers from what I observe …MoreHad to check it out after hearing good things about them.Was underwhelmed by the small portions. The pricing suffers from what I observe to be common for rib and pork joints in KL: always higher-priced, but not necessarily giving quality food/even value for money at allb. Go to Singapore's Tony Roma's and have their ribs and you'll know what I mean.The ribs here are okay. Not exactly melt in your mouth / fall off the bones immediately type. Based on what cooks tell me, it means that these guys may not be following the exact rib recipes which is often more time-consuming and costlier.My biggest beef with the place is the noisy ambience. There were a couple of birthdays and the staff would break into some unintelligible riot, umm, random sing song. Stressful and hugely discomforting: I called the manager over and he apologised for it, saying it was their way of celebrating their guests. I'm all for when in Rome and all that, but still...I didn't find Naughty Nuri's hugely compelling by any measure, and it's also pretty average. Author: Boon Loong Leo
Review: For the longest time I always thought that The Daily Grind serves only burger. Well, I was partly correct because they specialise in burger and particularly beef burger, although there are other options too. And that was what I got to taste …MoreFor the longest time I always thought that The Daily Grind serves only burger. Well, I was partly correct because they specialise in burger and particularly beef burger, although there are other options too. And that was what I got to taste (their other-than-burger dishes) when I didn't know what to eat after shopping in Bangsar Village.Even though they are located on Lower Ground level, the restaurant is actually visible from the main road. Note: I was actually utilising my Entertainer privilege (Buy 1 Free 1 main course) which was not applicable for burgers so we ordered mostly non-burger items except for one a-la carte item.◾O'mega Salmon - Pam seared salmon steak with light & tasty sundried tomato sauce & sauteed vegetables. Doneness was perfect and as requested. A good change using sundried tomato sauce.◾Rosemary & Lemon Roasted Chicken - perfectly half roasted chicken with sauteed tomatoes and charred romaine lettuce. They nailed this dish with tender and juicy chicken meat with some citrus note.◾Seafood Mac & Cheese - classic Mac & Cheese but with added fresh seafood. Added sweetness from the seafood incorporating with the cheesy sauce.◾Seafood spaghetti - gotta love the savoury garlic broth coating the Al dente spaghetti and fresh seafood.◾Classic chicken burger with cheese - reasonably thick and  juicy chicken party with melted cheese on top & a great set of buns, but not a breast in sight.I was pleasantly surprised with the quality of food here. Perhaps next time I'm back here again I'll try their non-beef burger.
Location: LG8 Bangsar Village, 1, Jalan Telawi, Kuala Lumpur

Author: JJ Lim
Review: Food - 3 starService - 1.5 starPrice - 2 star …MoreFood - 3 starService - 1.5 starPrice - 2 starFood was alright, not something extraordinary. The taste was just alright.Service was disappointing - *disclaimer maybe I am not familiar with these latest trends.Table occupation duration - I made a booking and found out that the table has a time limit, the table has been timed for 2 sessions, 6 pm to 9 pm (session 1) and 9 pm to 1 am (session 2). It was really not convenient for me, as my friends were only able to arrive soonest at 730pm.Customer service experience - maybe I wasn't in a good mood after getting a” time limit” for such meals. Therefore, I feel like the waitress wasn't sincere while serving us. Didn't look at us while talking, etc. Little act that made us didn't feel ”welcome”. Probably we had too many requests.Food serving experience wasn't good, we ordered Lou Sang before the rest of the food is served. First of all, the price of Lou Sang is 88 ringgit for 6-8 pax (to me it is expensive with what we get, as initially, we thought that we wanted to bring our own) and somehow the rest of the food that we ordered did not start to serve, or start preparation/cook. Apparently, the expectations are to only start preparing/cook the food after we finish our Lou Sang. Which... We are shocked about, as we are not aware that it's a common practice. Ending up, the rest of the food only to be served approximately 20 min (or that is roughly how long we felt) after we did Lou Sang.

Author: Edmund Chee
Review: A very fine place, with a luxurious touch to the atmosphere and the environment. This is one of the best, well decorated furnished bar and restaurant that I’ve ever seen. Love the jazzy music and the friendly staff members there! …MoreA very fine place, with a luxurious touch to the atmosphere and the environment. This is one of the best, well decorated furnished bar and restaurant that I’ve ever seen. Love the jazzy music and the friendly staff members there!Went for lunch, and got the set lunch menu. Chose the Carbonara Egg De Cloud and the Genki Curry Smoked Ribs.It’s rare to see restaurants with Egg De Cloud because it’s not something that everyone know how to make or even heard or seen it before. That became one of my favourite dishes there, for it’s fluffiness and creamy texture with carbonara mushroom cheese and corn beneath it.On the other hand, the Genki Curry Smoked Ribs is a total failure (advice not to get the dish). I would say it’s a dish that doesn’t suit the taste, because of the tasteless ribs that comes with a mushy grinded pork cake beneath a soft egg yolk. Too little curry, and the curry doesn’t taste good. It’s a very confusing dish I would say.Other than that everything seems fine. Though it’s location is slightly towards factories area, but it’s a worthwhile visit for pictures and relaxation.

Author: Cheah
Review: It was mediocre. I was expecting more considering the awards they've won in the past. However, it felt as if the chef was really mainly only focused on plating the food for aesthetics. Taste wise, there's nothing to complain but nothing …MoreIt was mediocre. I was expecting more considering the awards they've won in the past. However, it felt as if the chef was really mainly only focused on plating the food for aesthetics. Taste wise, there's nothing to complain but nothing memorable either. The wait staff did his best to explain each dish as it was being served but it was rather apparent he was just regurgitating what he was told to memorize and sounded really anxious. I don't blame him but I feel the entire dining environment was a bit try hard. None of the floor staff showed enthusiasm, knowledge or passion towards the food they were serving - sort of defeats the whole fine dining concept.I would have given a 3star rating, but i was mainly concerned with the hygiene and some of the little things I observed while dining there made me a little squirmish and uncomfortable. In a Covid-19 time like this, I would expect places to up their game on hygiene but was honestly slightly shocked to find that that cutleries were set directly on the table. I tend to be skeptical about the cleanliness of tables in general, so I actually got them to change my set.My side plate also had residual food stuck to it 😐 got them to change that too. Appetiser was served in a wooden tray of beans, with the amuse bouche placed directly on the raw beans. Again, in a time like this, I get a bit worried as to how clean this wooden box is, whether or not the same decorative beans were used to serve previous customers and if so, did any kid or patron previously digged their fingers and played around with these very beans. Perhaps I'm a bit paranoid but I tend to take notice on small things like this.I would have let it slide a little more if it was at a more casual diner, but given Skillet's reputation and the price you're paying, I expected a much higher level of hygiene.
Location: Unit 1, Lot 163, Retail, No. 10, Jalan Perak, Kuala Lumpur

Review: Let me deal with the negatives first. This restaurant will not give you a glass of table water unless you pay over 20MYR for 750ml. Even if you are 7 months pregnant, still no glass of water. Even if you are 7 months pregnant, still no glass of water. Now, I don't know about Malaysia, but where my wife and I are from, it is basic good manners to offer people a glass of water. But good manners aside, when people are spending 300MYR on dinner, and you are charging 10% for service, it is it scandalous to not offer a glass of water. It's simple greed.Now, that aside, the food was impeccable. Well done to the team of chefs, because every dish was both presented beautifully and cooked to perfection. The small touch of the 'mini dishes' between each course was fantastic. Food wise, Skillet is most definitely 5*.Let us know if you start giving people a glass of water and we'll be back for more of your wonderful food. Author: JLo
Review: For crab or seafood lover like me… although Alaskan crab always labelled as expensive but it is totally worth eating (the live one which are still filled with meat, not the frozen one). Mud crab nowadays are also quite pricey and the amount …MoreFor crab or seafood lover like me… although Alaskan crab always labelled as expensive but it is totally worth eating (the live one which are still filled with meat, not the frozen one). Mud crab nowadays are also quite pricey and the amount of meat that you can get from the Alaskan crab and ease of it is totally worth it. This is a winner to me even compare to the lobster in term of taste, flesh texture, and price. The chef recommended a few cooking style but just steam with mild shaoxing and soya sauce is good enough. Next dish will be the scallop with mince garlic, I can’t have enough of it. My family who doesn’t like garlic taste, find this very tasty too. Fish and prawn need not to say much as it is fresh and alive from the tank. Taste good for sure 😁. The vege is having a little bitter taste. Seafood claypot with sea cucumber, fish maw, clams and mushroom. How can a seafood lover pass that? 😋😋😋 The clam soup is very sweet too, because the clam is very fresh we requested not to put too much pepper or anything that will make it spicy. 👍🏻👍🏻
Location: LOT 9B-3, Jalan Kemajuan 12/18, Seksyen 13, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Julian Lee
Review: This place was said to be so notorious that you couldn't even get a table without a reservation. If you come here with high expectations of table service then I'm sorry to say you will be disappointed. Everything down to the cutlery and …MoreThis place was said to be so notorious that you couldn't even get a table without a reservation. If you come here with high expectations of table service then I'm sorry to say you will be disappointed. Everything down to the cutlery and water is self service. They have the kitchen floor and the air conditioned floors above. Take a board when you go in, order and fill in your table number. Get your cutlery and drinks; wait. Most will be turned off by this but the place let's you know its the food that does the talking not service. My first taste of Wagyu was mediocre to say the least. Since moving on from my teenage years, I never order well done. Today it's medium rare and It ticks all the spots- tender, juicy and no gaminess (cow smell) but that was it. I didn't feel the oomph or feel good factor and now sitting down, I think I've figured out why. I'm so used to the gamey smell that without it, I felt something was missing. This wagyu was devoid of the gamey smell which is the characteristic of the meat, hardly the fault of the chef. The 300g Wagyu Flank will set you back RM95 whilst the cheaper Wagyu Rump will set you back at RM85 which is not exorbitantly expensive if you have tasted kobe beef. Suggest those interested in a no frills dining, to reserve a table during dinner or walk in for lunch. Fame and fortune is around the corner do act fast. EDITED: they now have tableapp, you will need to go to Facebook, select the dates and pay RM15 deposit per person. Make sure you remind them about the deposit when paying for the bill.
Location: 608, Jalan 17/10, Seksyen 17, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Gifford
Review: Heard about this place from a friend with good things to say. Small and simple restaurant. As they say, where there's a crowd, good food follows. Tight seating arrangements to accommodate larger crowds but despite that, there was still a …MoreHeard about this place from a friend with good things to say. Small and simple restaurant. As they say, where there's a crowd, good food follows. Tight seating arrangements to accommodate larger crowds but despite that, there was still a long queue at the front.Hard to find of you don't know what you're looking for (poor signage), and if not for the long queue, wouldn't have spot the restaurant easily. Simple Western menu with simple instructions 🤣. Wanted the flank steak but sold out before could order. Went for the striploin, med rare. Best piece of steak I've had all year, well seasoned and juicy with perfectly cooked steak cut fries. Simply tantalizing. Green tea tart after to top it off, just hits all the right spots.In my opinion, not the best romantic setting for a date, more of a gathering of friends. The food is 5 🌟 in a 2 ⭐ environment. Affordable food, given the quality. But it could be, the environment is to create the illusion of exclusivity, due to the overwhelming crowd, well played. Definitely coming back for the tomahawk and Kobe in the near future.

Author: Sharon C
Review: Good food, decent prices, subpar service. If you like instagrammable food, this place is not for you. …MoreGood food, decent prices, subpar service. If you like instagrammable food, this place is not for you.However, our experience was clouded by what happened before we even sat down and ordered. We had a booking for 8.30pm but received a whatsapp at 7.50pm saying our table was ready early because the previous table ended early and that we could come earlier. We said ok and informed that we would be there in 10-20mins. We arrived 20mins later (8.10pm) but were made to wait 25mins (8.35pm) because, and i quote, "Your table is not ready yet." We would have not minded the wait had we not been told to come earlier. There was no apology for the mix up or the wait.Also, while we understand that Grub is a no-frills place that is all about the food, it would have been nice to be greeted with a smile at the reception in the very least. This lack of hospitality, together with the fact that we had to wait so long when told to come earlier put us in a less than ideal mood before we even sat down and ordered.That said, the flavor of all the food we ordered was amazing (wagyu NY strip steak, seafood soup, meatball marinara) except for the yorkshire pudding. The fish in the seafood soup was especially good - would highly recommend. The texture of the steak could be better, but no complaints about the food except that the seafood soup was lukewarm when it reached the table and the yorkshire pudding was incredibly oily.
Location: 608, Jalan 17/10, Seksyen 17, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Mitchell Lim
Review: U can call this place a hidden gem cos it's smacked in a residential area & behind a primary school. Restaurant has 2 floors. Booking is encouraged as it could be very pack during dinner. Booking system used is strictly TableApp only & a …MoreU can call this place a hidden gem cos it's smacked in a residential area & behind a primary school. Restaurant has 2 floors. Booking is encouraged as it could be very pack during dinner. Booking system used is strictly TableApp only & a deposit of RM10 is mandatory @ time of booking (payment via credit card).Their style of food is fusion. Menu for end October attached. For 5 adults, we had 1.5kg Angus Tomahawk & 300g Wagyu Striploin (that came some sides), a bouillabaisse + 5 additional sides.A reminder, it's a fusion restaurant. First, the bouillabaisse (5/10) was decent, seafood well cooked while broth tasted Asian w splash of white wine...nowhere near French or English fish stew.Medium steaks done truly medium (slightly pink) with au jus. Both pretty tender; just lack seasoning. Rate them 6/10. The sides were mediocre (no more than 3/10 overall). The Yorkshire pudding looked like one but taste & texture far from the real deal.Besides cooked food being served, everything else is self-service. They had a system meltdown on Sat..cashier + kitchen kinda chaotic...it's understandable but all staffs were very moody. In the end, manager did apologise on his own accord 👏🏻 The spot to place your order is the cashier. So, be prepared to really queue while helping yourself to free kitchen infusion Haha.Overall experience & food quality have plenty of space to improve.Given it's congested layout, it's not wheelchair & stroller friendly. Parking available in front of shops & along the neighbourhood playground (walking distance).

It was humongous, oily and cheesy beyond any burger I had in Malaysia, but the chips were tasteless. The most disappointing dish was the chicken satay. Author: Teoh Kheng Hong
Review: First time dine in this place. The environment is quite pleasant and comfy. The menu doesn't have picture so we couldn't get the size of the food when they arrived. Luckily the staff is kind enough to stop us from ordering too much. …MoreFirst time dine in this place. The environment is quite pleasant and comfy. The menu doesn't have picture so we couldn't get the size of the food when they arrived. Luckily the staff is kind enough to stop us from ordering too much.The food here are quite enjoyable.The Garlic Fries are freshly cut and fried paired with chili garlic aioli sauce. It's a very well combination as the quality of the fries is better than most fries out there dipped with good sauce(not your typical chili sauce).The most unexpected is the Halibut Fish and Chips as it's serving size is big enough for 3 persons. The thousand island sauce is well made and balanced as it doesn't overwhelm the taste of the fish when eating it. It also comes with light fried fries underneath the fish with a bowl of salad and it is one of the most valuable dish here.The Ultimate Bo+Co Meat Platter the most festive one and it is also very well made, but my naught complain is the meat ratio is quite unbalanced as the servings of the duck and beef is smaller than the chicken and mutton. The grilled beef flank and the smoked duck are very well made, Kudos to the chefs!For the drinks, the hot Cappuccino has a very defined design on it with good aroma. The Ice Blended Juice they have given me a flavor of Watermelon paired with Lychee, it definitely tasted better than any other sweet drinks as it doesn't over sweet like someone pour sugar into it.I definitely will come again with my family and friends for another festive meal!
Location: G5, 8, Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar South, Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur

Author: Wieke Veronicia
Review: Been there twice and always satisfy! From the service, ambiance and the foods! Loving it. …MoreBeen there twice and always satisfy! From the service, ambiance and the foods! Loving it.First time came for Brunch and second time came for Dinner.Here's my 2 cents for the Brunch menu:1. Southern Breakfast Fries, it says by Botanica + Co. I would say this is a creative and unique dish for a breakfast in Asia. It was a bowl of full baked shoestring fries, topped with creamy minced chicken velouté sauce and an over-easy fried egg. I could barely finish it, or I should say we had a hard time to finish this as it was fries mania. Not sure how western people could have this as their breakfast. It tasted good but I would probably recommend this to be a sharing platter.2. Spicy Fried Chicken Sandwich accompanied with freshly fried hand-cut chips. For its spicy heat, crispy texture yet juicy inside are something that I would love to eat again! Not to forget to their hand-cut fried chips, those are so lovely, thumbs up as it would let you forget about those commercial potato chips!Dinner menu:1. A warm, hearty, rich, filling, and silky smooth, creamy mushroom soup. The earthy flavours of mushrooms at its best pureéd into this soup along with sourdough toast. The Mushroom Medley Soup with Sourdough toast.2. A combination of soft brie, curd springy mozzarella, gritty parmesan, light and glorious ricotta in one pizza with porcini mushroom puree, rosemary, and muscatel (type of wine made of muscat grapes). Top pick for cheese lovers. Experience the bursting flavours in one bite.3. A just nice crust, not too thin or thick. Rustic edge, crispy with some chewiness dough in the middle. Perfectly served with its seafood fiesta with crab meat, prawn, mussel, squid on the authentic tomato based sauce, topped with oregano, mozzarella, and parmesan. Seafoods-cheese-licious with a hint of 🌿🍅 , sweet-savoury taste. Definitely my cup of tea kind of taste. A perfect harmony in my mouth. A must-try pizza.4. Mild-sweet white flakes, firm yet tender halibut fish wrapped in golden and super crispy batter one and only by Botanica+Co, Halibut Fish & Chips with bunch of steak fries underneath, tartar sauce and a bowl of refreshing vinaigrette salads on the side. Everything compliments each others. My best-pick dish of Botanica+Co for its worthy price.5. In the other hand, Botanica+Co brilliantly came out with this dessert called Churros Doughnut Stack, which a tower of guilty pleasure! It has crunchy exterior which resembles churros while inside is soft and moist like Doughnut. Then it leaves ice cream surprise inside which is protected in the ring, covered with torched-whipped cream and drizzled with caramel and chocolate.6. Presented as a familiar dish of apple crumble, this is a playful twist which came out as banana crumble. Structured from baked caramelised banana on the bottom to crumb mixture, topped with fresh seasonal fruits, sprinkled powdered sugar, and a scoop of vanilla ice cream. A perfect combination of hot and cold desserts such a great dessert on this season, ensuing a gleeful season!
Location: G5, 8, Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar South, Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur

Author: Wini Yeo
Review: Visited for the first time as friend commented it had decent food and good environment.Unfortunately it is one of the worst dining experiences I’ve had in KL. …MoreVisited for the first time as friend commented it had decent food and good environment.Unfortunately it is one of the worst dining experiences I’ve had in KL.Ordered pizza, wagyu burger, chicken burger and a pasta. Points for serving water within the first 5 minutes but it went downhill from there…Pizza arrived first after about 25 mins - we had to ask for plates and cutleries and was given  one less napkin… it was not easy to get the servers’ attention so as every Malaysian knows … self-service is the way to go…Drinks arrived 5 minutes after the pizza … yep food b4 drinks and the latte was meh. It tasted more like coffee flavoured milk.  However, it has decent latte fern art.The chicken burger arrived 10 mins later… and pizza was almost gone by then as we’d decided to share since everyone was really hungry by then. Then chicken burger was ordered later after server informed that the waffle was not available. So we thought the last two main meal should be ready soon.After 20 mins… Still no pasta nor burger. Asked the waiter to check…. Nothing after 5 minutes and was not given any response. Asked another waitress to check. No news after 3 minutes. That’s when we decided we’ve had enough.Went up to the counter to ask for the check as it’s ridiculous as diners who arrived later than us were getting their food but not us.Only then we we were offered a wagyu burger and a server explained that the kitchen didn’t get our order and were preparing the pasta. Found out also the burger was for actually for another table which they’d decided to give it to us first while they make us the pasta.  We couldn’t be bothered as the dining experience was already ruined.Pizza was decent and so was the chicken burger but disappointed that it did not come with fries(and I want to point out menu does not state fries).  For a $55 pizza and a $35 burger I expect better service. Will I return…? The food is not worth the poor service by a long shot.
Location: G5, 8, Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar South, Kuala Lumpur, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur

Author: Mark Leo
Review: Ambience and overall comfort are its strong suit. Food wise is a hit and miss. Amongst the food ordered within the group, the hits were the Laksa, halibut fish and chips and the Truffle fries. The laksa had a nice deep rich taste to the …MoreAmbience and overall comfort are its strong suit. Food wise is a hit and miss. Amongst the food ordered within the group, the hits were the Laksa, halibut fish and chips and the Truffle fries. The laksa had a nice deep rich taste to the broth and the truffle fries were nicely seasoned and it was crispy. The big breakfast was a big miss, scrambled eggs were overcooked and under seasoned. Mushrooms and tomatoes as well. Nothing really gelled well together as a whole. Cauliflower. This was a letdown for me because I felt the cheese and truffle were taking turns to overpower each other and it left me feeling slightly queasy. (Don't let me hinder your food choices though because my partner thought this was fine)5. Ambiance was horrible. I kind of felt like I was in a fish market because of how noisy it was.6. Author: Louise
Review: To be honest with my experience today, disappointed.Weekdays lunch set- …MoreTo be honest with my experience today, disappointed.Weekdays lunch set-Starter:👍🏼 Cauliflower - Quiche, diner likes it.👍🏼 Beef - Diner likes it.  🏼 Garden - Green salad, nothing too special, dressing is too strong and snapped all the attention (not in a positive way).🔺Their house made kombu butter is a highlight.Main:👎🏼 Chicken - Please skip this option, please. Taste very blunt, nothing extraordinary, understanding that chicken breast is naturally a hard-to-wow ingredient, sauce is not lighting the chicken up and almost taste nothing. Prawn ravioli doesn't look appealing and tastes bitter somehow! Couldn't taste the prawn either. Feedback to our server TWICE and made sure he delivered to the chef because it's **** for the price I paid. Sorry not sorry but Nando's is MUCH better.👎🏼 Lamb - Too dried, too- dried-. Jerky and chewy texture.👍🏼 Duck - Duck is tender! But portion/amount of the duck meat is far less to our expectation (3-4 pieces). Sauce is pretty good, diners like it.Dessert:👍🏼 Chocolate - Fresh baked soufflé, diners like it. Waited for a good while to be ready though.👌🏼 Apple - Tarte. Ordinary but good.👌🏼 Madeleine - Salted caramel ice-cream, nothing too wow unfortunately.I expected more for the price it costs per person. Would love to bring my (French) fiancé and his family here but I am reconsidering it after bringing my family here.Conclusion:🔺Atmosphere & Vibe: 🌕🌕🌕🌕🌗 (great to chill with friends and family for quality time)🔺Food & Taste: 🌕🌕🌖🌑🌑 (you need to choose VERY carefully and be lucky, not everything on the menu are impressive and worthy)🔺Service: N/A, no rating🔺Value: 🌕🌕🌗🌑🌑 (I can't get over the disappointment from my Chicken main course)🔺Safety (in the middle of a pandemic): 🌕🌕🌕🌕🌕 (all staff at front of house were wearing masks correctly, safe distance between tables and diners, paper bags provided for diners to keep their used masks)

Review: Food was lovely. Everything was well thought of. The chocolate souffle was so rich and creamy. It amazes me that their cheese selection is not much but the cheese that they serve on the platter was not that bad. Probably add some Colby Jack …MoreFood was lovely. Everything was well thought of. The chocolate souffle was so rich and creamy. It amazes me that their cheese selection is not much but the cheese that they serve on the platter was not that bad. Probably add some Colby Jack or a tiny serve of Parmigiano-Reggiano if it was up to me but it was actually good when paired with the crackers and grapes. The fish was exquisite. Im sorry to say but the hot chocolate needs to be thicker that it coats your mouth. I just prefer it the Italian way but props to the team at Entier for serving me and my family an amazing lunch. Thank you.

Author: CL Pui

Back to the food, everything truffled even the dessert, I got so bored of the same taste to be honest. And oh, they provide one set or cutlery only with tissue paper instead of proper napkin, and I need to cut my steak with a butter knife 🙃

Author: Hazel Thompson
Review: Amazing food with wonderful staff.The Chef makes a conscious effort to make French dining accessible and relatable …MoreAmazing food with wonderful staff.The Chef makes a conscious effort to make French dining accessible and relatable to the palate.The Chef's tasting menu is an eye opener, and for oyster lovers, the Iwagaki is NOT to be missed.I love the organic beet done 3 ways and the piece de resistance would be the wonderfully balanced meal plan and wine pairing symphony.Definitely worth a visit and especially so for a visit for a special occasion.The ambience is just the right touch of cosy and fancy with comfortably spaced seating in a simple elegant setting.In such extraordinary times, the thoughtful provision of a bag for your mask and the single use menu via QR code is much welcomed.

Author: Azlina Firzah Abd. Aziz
Review: Had the weekday set lunch with colleagues. One can have either 2 or 3 course selection at RM 69 & RM 88 respectively. Fantastic atmosphere on Level 41 with fantastic views of surrounding Bangsar. Fantastic menu with gorgeous plating, …MoreHad the weekday set lunch with colleagues. One can have either 2 or 3 course selection at RM 69 & RM 88 respectively. Fantastic atmosphere on Level 41 with fantastic views of surrounding Bangsar. Fantastic menu with gorgeous plating, attentive waiters and servers. Highly recommended! I would go again despite the not so perfect streets leading up to the hotel which is visible from Jalan Bangsar but requires a winding route to arrive at the hotel!!
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Cheng Joon Ng
Review: The food, ambience, views and service are Alla amazing. The waiters are knowledgeable of the menu and are very warm and helpful. Definitely come for an early reservation by the window so that you can enjoy the gorgeous view of the …MoreThe food, ambience, views and service are Alla amazing. The waiters are knowledgeable of the menu and are very warm and helpful. Definitely come for an early reservation by the window so that you can enjoy the gorgeous view of the surrounding area. As for the food, the nose to tail concept makes use of every part of the animal to minimise wastage, which is a philosophy more restaurants should adopt. The chicken heart and liver pate are highly recommended, along with the Josper lamb and baby cuttlefish. Basically, I was extremely satisfied with everything on the table, including the complimentary freshly baked bread.

Author: Johnathan Tan
Review: Overall great quality of food and relatively decent service. The sourdough bread as starters was great, including the seaweed butter. Also highly recommend their grilled leek and baby squid dishes. But the ultimate must try is their chocolate souffle. Absolutely decadent and chocolatey.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Karl Over
Review: Hits and misses...Some of the dishes were outstanding e.g mushroom tapas and the beetroot …MoreHits and misses...Some of the dishes were outstanding e.g mushroom tapas and the beetrootThe pricey truffle pasta was a let down. Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Vince Lim

Author: Chunchun Fang
Review: Food is great, service is fantastic. Lunch menu set starts at 58 for a 2-course menu which is very reasonable. The seafood and cuttlefish starters are good. Author: Annabelle Yoong
Review: Please make a reservation before you plan to dine in. Crowded.Corkage fees for wine RM100. And liquor RM200.Only certain dishes are worth trying, after tried almost the whole menu. But …MorePlease make a reservation before you plan to dine in. Crowded.Corkage fees for wine RM100. And liquor RM200.Only certain dishes are worth trying, after tried almost the whole menu. Author: May Jin

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Divakaren Nathan

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Nabil ZA
Review: Its a fusion french restaurant i would say. Very daring with the ingredients and most of it are local sourced. Food is excellent and we had a very nice and pleasant dining experience. Staffs were very attentive but i think they should …MoreIts a fusion french restaurant i would say. Very daring with the ingredients and most of it are local sourced. Food is excellent and we had a very nice and pleasant dining experience. Staffs were very attentive but i think they should recommend us dishes that are suitable for appetizers and mains as it was not stated in the menu. Their menu is basically divided into small, medium and large portions and the "whole/Entier" portion which is meant for sharing. It was pretty hard to guess and decide which dishes are suitable for a group of two.Will be back for sure to try other things.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Ralph Yong
Review: The restaurant is located high up on the 41st floor of Alila Bangsar. Entier French Dining is a modern chic and intimate looking restaurant that offers French fares that is not just classy but also very delicious that will make you …MoreThe restaurant is located high up on the 41st floor of Alila Bangsar. Entier French Dining is a modern chic and intimate looking restaurant that offers French fares that is not just classy but also very delicious that will make you reminiscing them over and over again.Entier means ‘whole’ or ‘entire’ in French which implement 5 senses culinary concept. The chef bringing in surprises to diners on the dining tables.Complimentary house-baked bread and kombu butter are really something to die for! The bread was served warm and it was soft and fluffy with crispy crust. It's so delicous that you could munched down more than half of the bread by your own!PRICE RANGE: RM 416 - RM 1,039CUISINES: French, EuropeanMEALS: Lunch, Dinner, Brunch, Drinks
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Maryn Jenkins Tan
Review: Some of the best food we've ever eaten. Absolutely loved it!
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Cheng See
Review: Probably one of the better French style restaurant in town. Food here is comforting and hearty, embraces a nose to tail menu of using off cuts to make very delicious and tasty food. Personally not alot of restaurants would take up this …MoreProbably one of the better French style restaurant in town. Food here is comforting and hearty, embraces a nose to tail menu of using off cuts to make very delicious and tasty food. Personally not alot of restaurants would take up this challenge to use more off cuts to pair in their food and make something truly delicious and special. Compliments to the chef in pushing the boundaries- Malaysia do need more restaurants like these to define and broaden our pallete from the traditional western food.The deco and view from the rooftop is amazing. I would recommend going for the lunch or early dinner option. Be seated by the window just to catch daylight and enjoy that beautiful view while dining. Food here generally cost around 100 to 150 ringgit per pax and cheaper for lunch sets.Recommended dishes1.) Chicken Heart, liver and skin on salad2.) Beef flank3.) Lamb poutrine4.) Octopus
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Wong Wai Cheng, Christine

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: subhabrata dasgupta

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Delyn デリン

Planning ahead, booking through their reservation website is …MoreLocated on level 41 in Alila Bangsar. There are two car parks - Valet parking or the multiple level car park at the back of Alila Bangsar. Entier is “usually” fully booked. Author: azura azman

Author: Benjamin Tan

Author: Jr Loh

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Shereen

Author: Shawn
Review: All in all a great experience and a great place for dates. Ambience was as amazing as the service and the food was relatively reasonable for the quality but lacking a little in terms of taste for the ox tail soup which I assumed would've …MoreAll in all a great experience and a great place for dates. Ambience was as amazing as the service and the food was relatively reasonable for the quality but lacking a little in terms of taste for the ox tail soup which I assumed would've been a thick broth but the dual texture from the tripe dish that had both crispy and braised tripe was amazing (the chinese in me wished there was rice to go with it as it can be a little overly seasoned) and the seafood was great in quality but in terms of preparation, it might have been too out of the box for my liking (for example the escargot croquettes) I'd very much rather have it with just the sauce.

Author: P Y Ng

Author: Elvin Leong
Review: Dined here in Nov 2021. Other than the bread (that was rly good), our mains and cauliflower were quite mediocre (I was disappointed as I was rly looking forward to try this place after hearing plenty of good reviews). Our food was also …MoreDined here in Nov 2021. Other than the bread (that was rly good), our mains and cauliflower were quite mediocre (I was disappointed as I was rly looking forward to try this place after hearing plenty of good reviews). Our food was also served abnormally quick (almost as if our food was pre-cooked) so I hope my experience was an isolated one. Heard from friends it was legit good before but standard had dropped.

Author: Eric Yap

Author: Adachi Yoshikazu

Author: Pui Yee Poon

Author: Chian Wern Tai
Review: Weekday set lunches at Entier French Dining is perhaps the most affordable french fine dining in town!Covid SOPs were strictly adhered to although restaurant was fully booked on a …MoreWeekday set lunches at Entier French Dining is perhaps the most affordable french fine dining in town!Covid SOPs were strictly adhered to although restaurant was fully booked on a weekday! Waiting staff wore face masks and face shield at all times. Tables and chairs were promptly sanitized.The food quality and presentation were amazing for the price. Highly recommended for special occasions!

Author: Adriana Amran
Review: Ambience is lovely, delicious food. Recommended
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Benson Eoh
Review: A very fantastic and unforgettable memories that Entier French Dining team have created for my family. …MoreA very fantastic and unforgettable memories that Entier French Dining team have created for my family.Staffs - The staffs are very polite, very professional and very gentleman. They greeted us when we entered and they took the chairs to allow the ladies to sit. They repeated each of the order and all the staffs are very well trained. We had 3 course meal today and they changed every single set of utensils when they are going to served the each of the course. They even wiped the table to make sure that it is clean before they serve another course of food. They even added the water by themselves without us asking.I called and make a reservation yesterday, they did ask me whether I have any special request and I said it's only for my mum birthday and since I doesn't have any solid plan yet I said just proceed with the table reservation and it's enough. However, during our dessert was served they even give us a surprise by giving us an extra dessert with Happy Birthday written on it. I am shocked with the surprise they have given to us ! Thank you so muchFood - Overall all the food is simply marvelous and scrumptious. The food presentation is as gorgeous as shown in the picture. The flavouring and the taste of the foods are very very deep. Several herbs are used to created the sauce as well. All the ingredients are very fresh as well.All and all my family and I do have an amazing lunch today. Thank you to the team and we will definitely be back again !
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: huiwen lai
Review: The food and ambience are totally worth the pay. Not to left out their excellent services which enlighten your dine in experience.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: mike leong

Author: Praveen Markandu
Review: Great dining experience. Food was very good and the prices were not overly expensive. The portions were pretty big as well. Ordered the Sawara Fish and the Forequarter Lamb Rack. For desert had the Chocolate Souffle. A word of caution, the …MoreGreat dining experience. Food was very good and the prices were not overly expensive. The portions were pretty big as well. Author: Chun Keat Chua
Review: Food and enviroment is nice .must try the caviar 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

Author: Wendy Wong

Author: Noushin Lee

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Izyan S.J
Review: Had dinner here. The service was exceptional. The ambiance is so romantic. The food.. Is the best! The salmon is fresh, and the lamb literally melt in your mouth. Very happy with the food. Def come back again!
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Alice Yong (AliceJomMakan)
Review: After hearing rave reviews, I have to try the food myself. No regrets as the lunch experience proves memorable. Social distancing SOPs enforced with tables set apart adequately. Great view of the Bangsar vicinity from the restaurant …MoreAfter hearing rave reviews, I have to try the food myself. No regrets as the lunch experience proves memorable. Social distancing SOPs enforced with tables set apart adequately. Great view of the Bangsar vicinity from the restaurant windows. Some of the dishes we sampled were excellent - inventive and noteworthy. The opener of beef tartare, main course of escargots with pasta and the madeleine dessert were showstoppers. My cauliflower-Comte cheese quiche and the lamb belly main weren't shabby either. Hats off to the chef and team for offering such a stupendously good, refined set lunch for less than RM100/head.

Author: Aim Aris

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Lu Lou
Review: scrumptious and satisfying recommended dishes with superb service.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: S M
Review: Great concept and yummy flavors.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Eric Siew Heng Lit

Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Hannah Ng
Review: Disappointing.Service was horrible, we ordered tapas, vegetable, fish, beef, lamb. …MoreDisappointing.Service was horrible, we ordered tapas, vegetable, fish, beef, lamb.Tapas, josper leeks & eggplant was service within 5mins of ordering. I can understand the tapas coming very quickly, but josper leeks in 5mins? Oh guess what it was cold. When i asked the waiters, Why do you service jospered leeks and egg plant as starters? They replied that those are starters. Like come on guys.The way the service us was also rushed. The dishes were just placed on ur table (messily) without explanation of what we were eating.We haven't even finished our food and the waitress were trying to collect everything like vultures.Our beef came quite chewy and difficult to bite, when ask if the part we chose was supposed to taste that way, the waiter replied. Yes if you not happy i will bring back to the kitchen 😬 can you even consider this "fine" dining?Tapas was so so, leeks was a disappointment. Fish was great. Service was a major disappointment. That we decided to skip dessert and headed to alexis.However i did see if you order the festive menu they take more care to explain, as seen from our neighbouring table.In conclusion, Entier is a great place for lunch. Just not dinner. It's also not a great place to expect any dining experience.I'd be back for lunch.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: D I

Author: VS

Author: Anee L.

Author: Sky suke

Author: Joo Siang Koh

Author: YX
Review: Here for the weekend lunch set menu and my overall comment would be - some hits and some misses. I’ve been here few times and overall I would say their signatures are good but the newer, more experimental recipes never hit the mark for me. …MoreHere for the weekend lunch set menu and my overall comment would be - some hits and some misses. I’ve been here few times and overall I would say their signatures are good but the newer, more experimental recipes never hit the mark for me.Kombu butter and corn bread is definitely wow. Daily marche soup is consistently good and the chocolate soufflé which is their signature was also always delightful.However their snapper ceviche really didn’t come together well at all (could barely finish it), didn’t really enjoy the treatment of the duck which was supposed to be like beef Wellington, and the clam pasta was plain at best (a normal spaghetti alla vongole wins anytime).The best main was the lamb belly which I didn’t manage to take a picture of - yes it’s a little gamey but acceptable for lamb. Taste was on point and melt in the mouth.The desserts from the set lunch also fell flat - banana eclair tastes good but the eclair texture was a bit dry. Mango crepe wasn’t great either.It’s a nice place to go to with great views and great design, but I would say just stick to a few signature items. There’s free flow coffee and tea that comes with the set too!3-course set lunch is at rm 125 (weekends)
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Ryan Liong
Review: Food - no nasty surprises from our orders for a table of 3. Lamb was done medium rare which was cooked well and that's all, and perhaps the fat can be further rendered just a bit more. Cauliflower is on the less interesting side but perhaps …MoreFood - no nasty surprises from our orders for a table of 3. Lamb was done medium rare which was cooked well and that's all, and perhaps the fat can be further rendered just a bit more. Cauliflower is on the less interesting side but perhaps it was meant to be a palate cleanser item. Eggplant had a smokey flavour but was a bit over-smoked for my liking. Escargot in a ball was a clever twist to make it less visually discouraging for one friend but friend no. 2 said the escargot is barely discernible. I'm neutral about it.Definitely not common of French cuisine to have chicken feet among other interesting choices that Asians are familiar with but not so much in the western world. Scoring an A for effort there! We saw our neighbouring table ordered the whole chicken and was struck with a case of food envy. Menu is pretty good for sharing.Price - you should expect the premium is in the ambiance, dining floor level, and renovation. Food alone, probably on the borderline high side for the simple ingredients, in line with the spirit of "entier". Not complaining here...Ambiance - went for dinner, lights were intentionally dimmed for cozy feeling but saw the other table struggling to read the menu under the lighting. Kesian the auntie.There are window seats for big groups, a nice touch.The interior work and finishing was well done. The place feels elegant and music choice was quite appropriate.Service - was largely attentive and professional. Remembered requests like serving coffee until after the meal. Commendable!3.5 stars from me. Google wouldn't let me do that half.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Solarance Shiro
Review: Went with my boyfriend’s family for the first time trying out the food here.. I’ll explain the food that I ate though since it’s only fair 😂 …MoreWent with my boyfriend’s family for the first time trying out the food here.. I’ll explain the food that I ate though since it’s only fair 😂1. I had the vegetable soup 🍲 and it tasted like creamy pumpkin with corn and it has a hint of truffle oil taste in it... it was quite sweet but good enough to keep on eating without feeling much sickness 😂 (I had lactose intolerance).2. I had the venison (Medium rare) and it’s served with pumpkin and beetroot purée. Honestly, the sauce is a bit salty but with the pumpkin purée which is just the right amount of sweetness to help balance the flavours of the dish overall. The meat is tender and pink on the inside and was flavoured perfectly.3. The dessert, we ordered the soufflé and the cheese cake. I’ll be frank, I’m not a big fan of the dark chocolate soufflé but my bf loved it and I trust his pallets. The cheese cake was amazingly light despite looking so thick. Tastes good too! But thanks to my stupid condition. I can’t eat too much of it lol... but it was a good experience.4. The service was quite attentive and since it’s uncle’s birthday, he got a free dessert which was made of strawberry and cheese. Basically cheese cake 😂 but it’s quite tasty and I actually preferred it over the desserts we ordered. It’s not much but just enough to satisfy me.So overall, the restaurant is great, atmosphere is comfortable and the view is great. But what I don’t like is it’s location since it’s near a residential area so the parking here is awful. Unless ur willing to pay RM 5 an hour, go ahead and park in the hotel. Otherwise, valet which is pricier.
Location: Level 41 at Alila Bangsar No 58, Jalan Ang Seng, Brickfields, Kuala Lumpur

Review: We dined here a couple of times for weekend brunch, dinner and lunch but all of the experiences differ as their service aren’t consistent. Firstly, we booked few days ahead in order to secure a place but only to realise upon arrival that there aren’t any tables available for us. Though it was peak period,  we saw 1-2 empty tables and yet they have to carry chairs and table from else where to place us in the middle of the restaurant which was crowded and distracting the flow of people passing by. Second, we requested an ala carte item that are not available on their set menu but they told us they can’t serve that to us as only set menu is allowed. However, we saw the table next to us was served the exact same item we requested. This is just too bias. Review: The place is a delight, great for money that offers a symphony of creative food, ambience and attentive service. We started off with the marche soup, salmon katsuo and mango prawn. The katsuo hit the spot (so says my son), and mango prawn …MoreThe place is a delight, great for money that offers a symphony of creative food, ambience and attentive service. We started off with the marche soup, salmon katsuo and mango prawn. The katsuo hit the spot (so says my son), and mango prawn was a miss (so says my husband). The fried baby corn with parmesan that came with the marche soup had a good crunch and the soup is a good old corn soup but failed to amaze me. We had the lamb belly and bavette steak as main. The bavette steak was really flavorful and done to perfection; we ordered a medium and a medium rare. The beef juice that came with the steak was so flavorful. Also like the parmesan fries that was served as a side. There is nothing to rave for the lamb belly. All the desserts were executed really nicely, the plating were feast to the eyes too.

I ordered a cake two weeks before and even confirmed that they offered drive thru services for the collection. I followed the …MoreI have been to their Bangsar store and the service was excellent. I followed the instructions and reached at the main entrance of ss15 courtyard where their store located. Placed a call to the store asking for sending the cake to my car. The first one who picked up said will check the order and get back to me. I received the WhatsApp message at my phone that they got my order and ready for collection. I thought of the waiter who answered the first time may have missed out the request to send to me at my car. So I called the second time, the guys said please hold for 5 mins as they are busy with dine in orders. I waited for 15 mins, I got nothing. I called for the third time, the staff asked me to drop by the store by my own as they are busy they can offer me drive thru collection. This is not an acceptable attitude. If you could not offer a drive thru service, you should not have shared with me and asked me to waited for such a long time before you asked me to drop by for collection. You must have thought, the person who responded the WhatsApp message may not be the ss15 courtyard store personnel! You are wrong, they have different WhatsApp number for different store. There is no such misunderstanding that the personnel who replied my WhatsApp doesn’t know abt the in-store situation! I paid for nearly 200 for a small cake and you give such services. Lousy!!

Review: I know I don’t visit this place as often as I like but every time I do, I am astounded by how good the food is.
