Best …MoreI know I don’t visit this place as often as I like but every time I do, I am astounded by how good the food is. So good in fact, that while going through my photos, I realise I always chow down the food before my camera gets to it. Best place in town if you feel like going carbless as they have paleo options available! I lovee their beef patty and would go with the paleo on a good day but on a bad one I’d eat their cheeseburger anytime any day (extra fries please 😂) The atmosphere is, without a doubt, awesome, and I love having my small get togethers here. We once had the party set buffet food for our party and everything was so good even in big portions! I still remember the taste of that fish burger slider and the bbq chicken too. Even though I was stuffed with spaghetti, they tasted so good on a full stomach which means it’s awesomely awesome. I love the way they are so good at using their ingredients to the best of its abilities as they are great at blending those tastes which makes the food taste different yet delicious. Hats off also not just to the kitchen staff but to their floor staff who are always friendly and helpful :)

Author: Vhailor
Review: Been hearing a lot of good things about this restaurant but never got the chance to personally dine there. I did attempt a couple of times but it was closed then. Saw that they were on Grab & decided to order in. Like the packaging, it's …MoreBeen hearing a lot of good things about this restaurant but never got the chance to personally dine there. I did attempt a couple of times but it was closed then. Saw that they were on Grab & decided to order in. Like the packaging, it's well planned. I ordered 2 items the saba curry risotto set & their premium gyoza starter. The starter apparently was sold out so I didn't receive it. The saba is a hit & miss for me. Some aspects of the fish I liked & there are aspects which I didn't. Portion size is ok ok. If u're a large eater this ain't gonna fill u up. What surprised me was I took my first spoon I was pleasantly surprised by an exploding egg yolk. It was carefully hidden under the vege and it was very awesome. Added a creaminess to the dish. The saba didn't look appetizing but it has a nice crispy skin and it was fresh so no real complaints here. My only gripe with the dish was the curry risotto. It was sadly under seasoned and I didn't get much of a curry flavour coming through. The accompanying tea was refreshing- nice. So to be fair I'm gonna give a average rating till I can actually get there and dine in for a proper rating. Will update later
Location: 19, Jalan 20/13, Taman Paramount, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: bull bar
Review: Technical evaluation report (ya. after eating, nothing to do):1) Meat: the meat seems like smoke meat before going for roast, never had this type of roast pork with smoke meat before, …MoreTechnical evaluation report (ya. after eating, nothing to do):1) Meat: the meat seems like smoke meat before going for roast, never had this type of roast pork with smoke meat before,2) Fat: the fat part could have more fat, but very much personal preference,3) Roast skin/lard: The roast skin is very good, if you like pork lard, this is crispy pork lard,4) Sauce: chili sauce is pure chili + some vinegar (i think), the traditional chicken rice chili sauce type,5) Packing: the 1kg roast pork should have come in one piece (FMCO, delivery), the heat can retain within the meat better, the crispy skin/lard can face up. copping in small piece is convenience but also make some pieces up-side down or side way, the juice at the bottom container has made the crispy skin soft. let customer choose whole piece or cube, i will prefer reheat whole piece in oven.6) overall rating: google 4 star (improve packing or give customer choose 1 piece or cube will give you 5 star)suggestion: make small pieces all face up in a container with satay stick string them together, ya more work but  your customer will be happier.
Location: 75, Jalan SS 22/19, Damansara Jaya, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

All in, quite satisfying. Will be back.

Review: This restaurant was recommended by a good friend of my sister and it didn't disappoint. Parking was not difficult. Restaurant has a AC and non AC section. The shop is popular so it's better to call and book a spot in advance to avoid …MoreThis restaurant was recommended by a good friend of my sister and it didn't disappoint. Parking was not difficult. Restaurant has a AC and non AC section. The shop is popular so it's better to call and book a spot in advance to avoid disappointment. This place is know for their crabs. They charge per crab and not per kilo as some other places. Small crab is rm35 per piece. But bigger ranges 100-130 depending on weight and I think they have even bigger sizes but prices goes up as well. Food was very tasty and we enjoyed ourselves immensely but retrospectively looking at the bill I would say perhaps the fish slice and chicken were expensive for the portion sizes. So I guess if u come here focus on the crabs it's really good. Ambience is basic. It's basically a low frill experience. Definitely will come back again

Author: N Y
Review: Looking for a local crab eatery and found this on Internet. It was not crowded yesterday when we went at 6.15pm but by 7.15 the restaurant was almost full. There is an aircon section where we were seated. A very authentic, old Chinese …MoreLooking for a local crab eatery and found this on Internet. It was not crowded yesterday when we went at 6.15pm but by 7.15 the restaurant was almost full. There is an aircon section where we were seated. A very authentic, old Chinese restaurant that looks more like a coffee shop. Waitress was friendly and was keen to offer the big crab option. When we accepted the offer, she looked happy. She later came in and show us the price tag for the crab which was 151 rm for 1. Apparently Big crabs are individually priced and you could choose it at the entrance of the restaurant in some styrofoam box. So this restaurant is an honest restaurant.  Crab was meaty, fresh ,tasty and full of gravy ( we ordered butter crab ) i needn’t have ordered extra sauce. Personally I would have preferred more chilli padi inside. I compared our crab with the standard  crabs that others ordered, they are about 2 times the size. Lala bee Hoon is good but not fantastic. Upon settling bill, I asked about the standard crabs, was told that their price is 2 crabs for 104 rm. I will surely come again, and I think I will go for salted eggs crab next time. A nice, happy  and yummy dinner!
Location: 34, Jalan BM 1/2, Taman Bukit Mayang Emas, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

We ordered "man tou" which are fried buns to savor the delicious gravies that comes with these crabs dishes. If i recall correctly, we have ordered about 6 plates of fried buns to savor both the chillies and butter crabs dishes. On top of all, the crabs served were really fresh and they were cooked with perfection. The only dish that I am not satisfied was their fried pork meat because it was a bit dry. I would recommend crabs dishes for sure because that's what thry are famous for. Really had a super satisfying dinning experience here. Do give your view on their foods next time you decided yo visit the place.

This was the same guy who threw the cutlery into the tray when we asked for new ones. This was my third visit to Lankan cafe and I feel the service has deteriorated.

Review: 15 Dec 2020 - I ordered the mutton Briyani, thosai and 2 paal apam and unfortunately the apartment arrived first. I dont know if they understand the idea of dessert but it did not matter, as I wolfed it down in 2 seconds. And it was piping …More15 Dec 2020 - I ordered the mutton Briyani, thosai and 2 paal apam and unfortunately the apartment arrived first. I dont know if they understand the idea of dessert but it did not matter, as I wolfed it down in 2 seconds. And it was piping hot mind you. The apam was really good. Soft center and crispy on the outside. The thosai was generally of Average fare. The dhall was the heavy yellow type lacking in spice and very bland. The mutton Briyani was very good. Very spicy with tender pieces of mutton served separately, with some gravy and salad. Service was attentive.March 2018 - I dropped in to try out their buffet advertised at rm16.90 per pax. The spread was basic with i chicken curry as the meat dish. Sothie, long beans, patola were essentially the main offering (check out my pics) eaten with either rice or string hoppers. The chicken was ok. So too the sothie. I took on the brown sugar appam (seperate order) and that too was just ok.I would like to return to try their Lamprais which is available only on weekends and public holidays. Service was good although i was the only one there.

Author: Wyyippy Py
Review: Nice halal dim sum place for people who are working around KL Sentral area. The food standard is great given the cooking and ingredient is halal. Serving is fast if you were to be here ahead of the lunch crowd, say around or just past 12 …MoreNice halal dim sum place for people who are working around KL Sentral area. The food standard is great given the cooking and ingredient is halal. Serving is fast if you were to be here ahead of the lunch crowd, say around or just past 12 noon. Otherwise, one might need to wait between 15-30 mins for a seat. Food arrived steaming hot and right temperature for dim sum. The menu is not a long list but contain most of the popular dim sum dishes; well enough for an enjoyable working lunch. Price is reasonable. Going by the chinese translation of the restaurant name, abundant profit, the price is reasonably commensurate with quality of food. As comparison vs Chyna at Hilton or vs China Treasures of Sime Darby Convention Centre; this place is as good if not better - at least value for money! If you are at KL Sentral and looking for good halal dim sum, this would be my recommendation.

Author: A L
Review: We had an enjoyable tapas dinner at Marta’s. Food standard and taste were maintained well above average. The Galician octopus was average but not bad, texture was alright but lacked flavour. Iberico Manchego platter was good and paired well …MoreWe had an enjoyable tapas dinner at Marta’s. Food standard and taste were maintained well above average. The Galician octopus was average but not bad, texture was alright but lacked flavour. Iberico Manchego platter was good and paired well with the tomato spread bread. Prawns in garlic and evoo is always a must-order; prawns cooked perfectly flavourful. Iberico ribs were off-the-bone tender, juicy and yummy; definitely one of the signature must-order. We also ordered another special— stewed iberico pork cheeks in red wine sauce. The meat was very tender and the sauce taste excellent but portion could be slightly larger. Croquetas with jamon and bechamel sauce was really yummy it’s a must-have. Paella Valencia was also really flavourful with rich taste of the seafood broth. Rice texture could be slightly more firm but overall still comforting and delicious. Churros were freshly prepared, more on the crispy side and less chewy but taste good. Basque burnt cheese cake was too sweet for our palette. Service could be slow as expected due to the large crowd especially at peak hours hence advice to book ahead and arrive early. Still one of the best places in town for good selection of drinks and authentic Spanish food.
Location: Jalan Sri Hartamas 22, Desa Sri Hartamas, Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Author: Boon Loong Leo
Review: Spanish Tapas at its finest!There are not many Spanish tapas restaurants that you can find in Klang Valley - …MoreSpanish Tapas at its finest!There are not many Spanish tapas restaurants that you can find in Klang Valley - after taking out those which do not serve pork such as La Bodega, Cava, La Cocina,.. the choice got lesser. Tapas Club has closed down for good during MCO which was a bummer because I love this place. Mercat Barcelona doesn't have the kind of warmth and elegant feel to it..Then there is Marta's Kitchen - located in The Signature Hotel & Serviced Suites in Desa Sri Hartamas, they have been doing quite well themselves.. from a smaller outlet, they have since moved to a bigger one directly opposite from where they used to be. Service team was well-trained to take orders in timely manner, and friendly too.◾Patatas Bravas RM19 - Classic Spanish tapas where the potatoes are deep fried before smothered with creamy aoili and their signature spicy tomato sauce.◾Pinchos morunos RM20 - marinated pork kebabs mixed with spices from Spain. So soft and tender that the pickiest eater of all couldn't stop eating it.◾San Jacobo RM28 - tender piece of pork sirloin filled with iberico ham and melted manchego cheese. My least favourite but credit to them, the meat was quite tender to begin with.◾ Pimentos asados con Arun (Ortiz) - marinated roasted pepper topped with tuna. For RM32, the soft and aromatic roasted pepper was topped with tuna and both combined well together.◾Valenciana paella RM37 - love the fact that I could order one portion, and that portion could easily feed 2-3 small eater.◾squid ink paella RM37◾juicy iberico pork ribs (half slab) at RM85 - fall-of-the-bone texture and well marinated pork ribs.. I couldn't stop munching on them! Paired perfectly with the red wine.◾Churros with chocolate RM17 - one of the most ordered dish in this restaurant. They have one section dedicated for this Spanish dessert.◾Basque cheese cake RM17 - although on the high side, their rendition of this by-accident dessert was flawless.◾cafe latte◾Sangria - there's this normal Sangria and then there's another one that made with champagne.If you think that the food is actually too much.. I had to clarify that I had gone there 3 times in a space of a month - 2 dinner and 1 coffee + cake sessions - just because I simply love most (if not all) of the dishes that I'd ordered. Coming here on weekends without reservation is not recommended unless you do not mind to wait.
Location: Jalan Sri Hartamas 22, Desa Sri Hartamas, Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Review: I dropped by just after lunch next door for dessert. After reading all the rave reviews about their cheesecake,  I just had to give it a try. I mean, it must be that good to have their own takeaway cheesecake counter near the entrance of …MoreI dropped by just after lunch next door for dessert. After reading all the rave reviews about their cheesecake,  I just had to give it a try. I mean, it must be that good to have their own takeaway cheesecake counter near the entrance of the food area.Matcha and the original cheesecake come with a large dollop of whipped cream on the side. My first impression. The cake is dense and has the texture of an ice cream cake but only much smoother and finer.Once inside the mouth, it just melts away, like ice cream. The pinch of whipped cream makes the cheesecake extra smooth. Never before had I tested such an amazing cheesecake. The fineness of it, the texture, and the well-balanced fresh ingredients just make you want to come back for more.I will and not for just the cheesecake but their main meals because the pricing is very affordable for the quality of food you will be getting.

Author: Zahirah Ardy
Review: Honestly speaking, I could neither find any fault in them nor did I want to. A soft gooey center to thick firm borderline bready edge of its burnt cheesecake, extremely smooth with a dollop of whipped cream. Paired with a scoop of …MoreHonestly speaking, I could neither find any fault in them nor did I want to. A soft gooey center to thick firm borderline bready edge of its burnt cheesecake, extremely smooth with a dollop of whipped cream. Paired with a scoop of hands-down the best burnt caramel ice cream I have ever had so far! We could taste the "burnt caramel" in that, perfectly infused. Ice cream was not icy at all (been having this issue with a major gourmet ice cream chain which can be due to inconsistent storage or faulty processing leading to ice crystals forming in rich cream-based ice cream)On further note, I enquired the chef if their sushi rice is made with mirin, a Japanese sake - which is common especially in kitchen of higher end Japanese restaurants - and was gladly informed that they use vinegar and sugar. Nil mirin. So sushi soon! Yay! And I was also informed that they do not use pork or lard. They also do not include alcohol as part of their food productionVerdict: Solid 5/5. Food is generally not so Instagrammable. Yes, we eat with our eyes, but this is legit strong well balanced flavours!
Location: level 4 LOT 10 SHOPPING CENTER, 50, Jln Sultan Ismail, Bukit Bintang, Kuala Lumpur

Author: Alan
Review: I am a sucker for beautifully designed spaces that offers great food. Uroko embodies all that and has quickly elevated itself to my favourite place for dining. …MoreI am a sucker for beautifully designed spaces that offers great food. Uroko embodies all that and has quickly elevated itself to my favourite place for dining.Uroko is located on the second floor of a corner shop lot in fashionable S17, Petaling Jaya. The restaurant itself is actually quite spacious, with a dining area that currently sits 28 (CMCO), a sushi bar area that sits 5 and there is 2 private rooms. Not forgetting, they also have a sake bar.The interior is beautiful. The space is fitted with custom made furnitures that is both beautiful and comfortable. The lighting is appropriately dimmed to create a cozy environment. The wall is fitted with gold matted fixtures adding a touch of class and sophistication to the space.But a beautiful interior is not the only thing.The food is delicious, beautifully presented and the quality is exceptional. The menu is filled with a range Japanese favourites. However, the real gems in the menu are dishes that have been infused with creative twists. I spotted a dish infused with foie gras and a carpaccio inspired dish in the menu.Our starter, the Salmon Carpaccio was extremely flavourful and colourful.The salad was visually appetising with its vibrant array of colour (Salmon dusted with truffles paired with crunchy greens), taste and textures.The Mars Maki was another delightful taste sensation combining a frittered spicy maki (did they add a hint of chilli padi into the maki?) with eel and tobiko.Our last dish was the Take Sushi platter. It came with 3 pieces of Uramaki and 6 different Nigiri Sushi plated from the lightest to the smokiest, left to the right.Sadly, one thing in the menu we did not get to try today, was the their Japan Amera Tomato. It costs RM35 a pop and I heard that it was divine.
Location: 2nd Floor, No 22A, 1, Jalan 17/54, Section 17, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Joon Ong
Review: About Menya Shi Shi Do:Mainly sells ramen in Tonkotsu broth (pork bone soup). There's green onion, black fungus, Japanese soft boiled egg and Char Siew (Roasted Pork slice) in the …MoreAbout Menya Shi Shi Do:Mainly sells ramen in Tonkotsu broth (pork bone soup). There's green onion, black fungus, Japanese soft boiled egg and Char Siew (Roasted Pork slice) in the ramen. It has different soup flavours such as soy sauce, basil, curry and etc. You can also combine up to 3 flavours in a bowl of ramen. Not to forget it has some special ramen such as Aka Oni (extra spicy ramen), iipon Yaki Buta Tsukemen (big slab of roasted pork) ramen.About the environment:The design are pretty Japanese authentic with red lantern and comic book pages on the wall. Seats are... ehm normal wooden seats you can get from Ikea. Do expect a long queue during peak hour. The distance between seats are far enough during covid-19 pandemic.About the dish:The soup is smooth and mildly rich with sweet flavour of pork bone, slightly smooth texture of collagen. Noodle is chewy and absorbed the taste of the soup enough to be flavourful. Japanese soft boiled egg has runny york which is good but the outer egg doesn't have enough flavour, as if the egg is not marinate long enough by looking at the colour. Black fungus are crunchy and flavourful itself and with the soup. Char Siew is thin and rich in flavour, the saltiness makes it stands out from the soup in a good way. The fat level of Char Siew is low but still very easy to chew.Conclusion:I love the richness of the soup where you can still finish the whole bowl without feeling greasy. With RM22-26 a bowl, it is a reasonable price and I would love to revisit and try some of their special ramen. 4-4.5 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐

So naturally …MoreThe double flavoured soups left a huge mark on me. As a fan of spicy food, my ramen needs to have a tinge of spicy for me to enjoy as it would normally be a little bit too “jelak” for me when I come to the end of a plain soup. So naturally we picked the spicy+basil combo, and the spicy+garlic combo and I must say the combination of flavours were really good. The spicy+basil is a little less flavourful and fragrant compared to garlic, but I love it because it’s so clear and simple and the flavours don’t hit you hard. The spicy+garlic had that garlic punch if you are a garlic lover. Oh and not forgetting the croquettes! Author: CS Lim
Review: Food was hit and miss. I wish I had read reviews before coming. My papardelle pasta with lamb ragu was a huge disappointment. The pasta was too thick. The sauce was bland and watery like a stew. I asked for cheese but it didn't help. The …MoreFood was hit and miss. I wish I had read reviews before coming. My papardelle pasta with lamb ragu was a huge disappointment. The pasta was too thick. The sauce was bland and watery like a stew. I asked for cheese but it didn't help. The only good thing is that my sister said she had a worse ragu pasta at another Italian place so this was a step up. Lucky me. The fettuccine with scallops and mushrooms in cream sauce was 5/10. Consistency of the sauce was good but underseasoned. The scallops lacked flavour.The antipasti misti made up for the disappointment. The pizza toppings were generous. The chicken hawaiian pizza was flavourful but the pepperoni was nothing special in comparison. The alla risata pasta is probably great for the seafood flavour but I try to stay away from things cooked in foil.I appreciated the bread basket with the olive tapenade but some of the bread was stale.Service was nothing to shout about. My family warned me that it would be slow and unresponsive so I guess my expectations were met. The air-conditioning didn't seem to work and the noise level was too high for my partner to participate in the conversation from the end of the table.I have such mixed feelings about this place because I used to love it in the late '90s. It has always been a little pricey, which is why I stopped coming when I had my own proper kitchen. I guess when we want good Italian food, we'll have to stick with cooking it ourselves.
Location: 128, Jalan Kasah, Medan Damansara, Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Author: Boon Loong Leo
Review: What's your favourite Italian restaurant? Honestly for me, Italian restaurant is not authentic Italian without pork (this sentiment is also echoed by most of my makan Kaki). That's why my favourite remains Verona, Sapore & Neroteca - just …MoreWhat's your favourite Italian restaurant? Honestly for me, Italian restaurant is not authentic Italian without pork (this sentiment is also echoed by most of my makan Kaki). That's why my favourite remains Verona, Sapore & Neroteca - just to name a few!However if there's one Italian that doesn't serve pork, yet I still go back from time to time.. it'd be La Risata. Something about this restaurant that makes me keep coming back - and it's their consistency and value pricing of their food.◾Zuppe Di Pesce (seafood soup) - available in half or full serving, I always go for the former as it's big enough to serve 2-3 person. It is actually a herb-spiced tomato broth served in a rather big bowl, filled generously with seafoods such as mussels, fish chunks, clams, prawns and squids. Did I mention that they spiced up the broth using bird eye chilly? It's a touch of genius I'd say, given that I love eating my chilly.◾Funghi alla Griglia or their grilled fresh champignon mushrooms with garlic. Gotta love the earthy taste of this dish.◾Pizza Margherita - featuring tomato, mozzarella, oregano and basil, nothing beats classic Italian style pizza done right.◾Agnello alla Griglia RM59 - grilled Australian lamb loin with fennel and black pepper crust, served with mashed chickpea & vegetable capitans finished with a red wine reduction. One of my favourite dishes in this restaurant - just the loin and the sauce, I don't know how to appreciate chickpea mash. Lol◾ spaghetti alle Vongole - the awesome pasta was cooked with clams in garlic, chilly and white wine sauce.◾ Spaghetti Alla Risata - a house specialty, imagine a hearty mixed seafood in hearty spicy tomato sauce... Baked in aluminium foil. Taste-wise similar to the seafood soup but with addition of spaghetti.◾ House Wines by the glass are available or if you're in the mood, go for the bottle.Instead of a fine dining eatery, it is in fact a rustic and casual Italian restaurant patronised mostly by locals. Coming here without any reservation on weekends is almost impossible so be smart and call ahead ya. To me, it's a money well spent especially when I get to eat quality food like these.
Location: 128, Jalan Kasah, Medan Damansara, Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Review: Quirky ambience with nostalgic and upcycled bric-bracs setting the stage for Thai BBQ and street food offerings. The som tum passes muster while the crispy oyster omelette - resembling a flat coral tableau - is an impressive sight, comes studded with a few oysters and needs a good lashing of the sweet chilli sauce to perk it up. Author: Cryssyenddo Xiang
Review: My wife and I wanted to have a dinner here after a long day at work. When we arrived at the restaurant, there was no one waiting at the entrance. The waiters saw us standing at the restaurant entrance, waiting to be seated, and they ALL …MoreMy wife and I wanted to have a dinner here after a long day at work. When we arrived at the restaurant, there was no one waiting at the entrance. The waiters saw us standing at the restaurant entrance, waiting to be seated, and they ALL pretended that we weren't there.Finally after a long moment of waiting (we really wanted to have our dinner there), this waiter named Yogesh (he refused to spell it although he had no problem telling me his name) finally appear from somewhere behind in the restaurant. He told us wait for 15 minutes (sounded like 50 minute, he can't even pronounce fifteen properly).I noticed an empty table in there, there was absolutely no one sitting there. I then pointed out the table and asked politely if we can take that table. He simply replied,loudly, and very impatiently: "We know what we're doing, just give us your name and wait for your table". I was baffled by the rudeness. I was merely suggesting, and this is the kind of attitude I get.This is the 3rd time I've visited this premise. Every single time, I'm met with waiter / waitress with various level of rudeness. No wonder there are not many customers visiting this premise in the normal hours. I suppose the occasional crowd didn't visit the premise that often. It makes me wonder too if Chili's trained their employees at all.In the end I decided this is the last time I'm taking this kind of attitude from Chili's. Suffice to say we've banned the restaurant indefinitely.We ended up having our dinner in Delicious instead. You can see that although those waiters are quite tired, they kept they smiles and served us to satisfaction.

Author: Giselle Hoo
Review: If I could give it 0 star, I would.Upon our entry, only serve us with normal menu and did not give an option for the lunch set menu. Only because we know and asked for it they gave us the option. …MoreIf I could give it 0 star, I would.Upon our entry, only serve us with normal menu and did not give an option for the lunch set menu. Only because we know and asked for it they gave us the option.Food prices increased, portion decreased. I know it's set lunch but come on, it couldn't be this small for 26++?Next thing, my Malay colleagues who have legit reasons of not fasting got kicked out of the place because "they will go to jail". With all due respects, the rule does apply (still disagree with the logic, but already make peace with such thinking) definitely only for those who are supposed to fast. Seriously you taking away people's rights to eat out when they are not fasting with reasons? (Even if no reason, it should be completely based on faith and not coercion but that would be out of topic.)Quesadillas felt like Tacos, crispier than crackers when it's supposed to be slightly soft. Inside of the tortilla skin was almost empty, with slices of chicken so pathetic might as well not put it. Black bean side was too salty, only tasted one bite. My friends fries were clearly frozen and stale, dryer than my face (usually I'm not even a picky fries eater!)In short, bad food, expensive, bad experience. Used to be a place I look up to and save for fancy occasion, but will never return.

Review: I am a really big fan of After Black. It is definitely my go-to place for honest and delicious food, as well as coffee. Their staff are highly attentive and so friendly. The place has a nice atmosphere for socializing and working. (Wifi …MoreI am a really big fan of After Black. It is definitely my go-to place for honest and delicious food, as well as coffee. Their staff are highly attentive and so friendly. The place has a nice atmosphere for socializing and working. (Wifi could be better but I think a lot of people were using it.) I have had a pleasant experience every time. Their coffee is lovely and I can be quite fussy when it comes down to that. Unlimited free water is a big plus, they make a conscious effort to reduce their plastic use which we respect so much! Last time I visited I had the mushroom open sandwich and WOW. I really enjoyed this a lot. Gorgeous flavors and the portion size is really acceptable. Their aglio olio is also super yummy. My bf goes for the fish and chips and loves it, although the portion for fries is a little small. You can tell the food is fresh, it comes out hot and has a lot of care put into it. Overall love this place so much and recommend it. There are some other cafe's along this street which do not compare whatsoever. We will be back!

Ordered a filet mignon sous vide, cost about 80-90 dollars, thinking it's not a …MoreAfter seeing good reviews on the internet, decided to try out this restaurant. Service was ok, as it was Father's Day, knowing the restaurant would be a busy. Ordered a filet mignon sous vide, cost about 80-90 dollars, thinking it's not a bad cut, however, it came out disappointing. i have had much betters ones for less the cost elsewhere. the steak feels  dry & not juicy,  but sides were good in comparison, the potato, vege etc. i left half of the steak portion untouched, as i couldn't find myself finishing eat. We also ordered another filet mignon steak(not sous vide), to be done medium rare, however the steak came out either came out well done or raw.  We have returned the steak thrice (and neither one came out even at medium, so medium rare seems actually impossible for them) and we finally changed the order. As the steak cut was  relatively thick, the cooking technique doesn't seem to correspond to the thickness hence the outcome of a medium cook steak was impossible for them. If i were Gordon Ramsay, i would go into the kitchen and get the steak done myself. Also, the highly raved mushroom soup was good, however too salty, hence a point to take note of.Response from the owner:Hi , from our record there was non of the sous vide steak remain unfinished from all our customersfrom that night, as v only served 4 tables sous vide steak on that night . If u …MoreMy response:The feedback is not about whether my steak has remains or not, as it's not relevant to the topic, but what i'm trying to make a point here is that this restaurant is not capable to produce a "medium" filet mignon (a relatively thick cut), a point to note off.

The mushroom gravy given is a big No, watery & rather bland. Steak not well marinated & not succulent. So, not meet my liking. They are very willing to help you take photo whenever you request. But, after we hv make the payment & walked out, suddenly another staff come after us & asked whether we hv ald pay the bill. Author: thai69girl
Review: The atmosphere was romantic, next to the lake, lighting and the sort. It was disappointing that they ran out of crawfish which was suppose to be the signature of a New Orleans restaurant. Later they also ran out of large mineral water. …MoreThe atmosphere was romantic, next to the lake, lighting and the sort. It was disappointing that they ran out of crawfish which was suppose to be the signature of a New Orleans restaurant. Later they also ran out of large mineral water.We ordered a seafood gumbo. It didn't look like a gumbo, but nonetheless it was quite tasty as it was full of cheese. The escargot was again cheesy. I think they ran out of parsley as the garnishing was only julienne of carrot.  The oyster rockerfeller was a bit of a let down as the oysters were a wee bit small as such we could not get the natural taste of oyster. The Creole seafood platter was fair. Again there was no crawfish so more prawns. Which was fine but when the pasta which was the Fortune of Neptune can it was more or less the same with the seafood platter only with pasta. In place of the Boil Crawfish we ordered the Rank of Lamb. It came cut which was rather disappointing. The lamb was over cooked and salty.Overall it was an average experience. The service was slightly below par but I suppose they were focused on an event.
Restaurant: Secret Of Louisiana at the lake
Location: D - 01, 1, Jalan SS 7/13a, SS7, Petaling Jaya, Selangor

Author: Deborah W.
Review: I've been a loyal customer for the past 4 years, but this time is really bad. No doubt the food, esp the carbonara is good, but the waiters are horrible! Service was bad during buffet lunch, and I spent more time waiting for my food than …MoreI've been a loyal customer for the past 4 years, but this time is really bad. No doubt the food, esp the carbonara is good, but the waiters are horrible! Service was bad during buffet lunch, and I spent more time waiting for my food than eating the food! The waiters were so blur and didn't know who ordered what, and they didn't write orders down so as they go on and take orders from other tables, they forget the older ones. Had to call so many times just for the bill, and they came back with the bill and 1 ice cream for 2 people when it's supposed to be 2 ice creams. Told the guy to bring another one since it's 2, but ended up still waiting for such a long time that we decided to forgo it since there was no mood to eat anymore with such bad service. It's really a drastic change from how it used to be last time, where even though they were busy, they were systematic and even proactive to ask whether you want more pasta or refills. Really disappointed with the new crew.
